document.masterDocTitle: A revision of the genus Leptotyphlops in northeastern Africa and southwestern Arabia (Serpentes: Leptotyphlopidae)

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: A revision of the genus Leptotyphlops in northeastern Africa and southwestern Arabia (Serpentes: Leptotyphlopidae)

subSubSection.paragraph:
Leptotyphlops braccianii
(
mean based two males , BMNH 1905.11 .7.41,
MCZ 40089
, and two females ,
MCZ 52190
, 53330 )

Posterior tip of sternohyoideus 13.5%, sternohyoideus-heart gap 0.09, heart 4.2% (MP = 16.7%), snoutheart interval 18.8%, heart-liver gap 8.1%, liver lobes multipartite, right liver 42.8% (MP = 48.3%) with 47.3 (34–57) segments, left liver 38.0% (MP = 47.9%) with 41.3 (35–47) segments, a zig-zag postcaval vein, anterior liver extension 0.06, posterior liver tail 0.07, left liver = 0.88 right liver, liver-gall bladder gap 0.6%, gall bladder 1.5% (MP = 70.9%) adjacent to pancreas (1.8%) and caudal to juxtasplenic body (1.8%), testes multipartite, right testis 3.7% (MP = 82.9%) with 11.5 (11–12) segments, left testis 4.2% (MP = 85.7%) with 11.0 (9–13) segments, right ovary 3.7% ( MP = 75.6%) with no ova and 14 follicles, left ovary 5.3% (MP = 79.8%) with 1.5 (0–3) ova and 9.0 (6–12) follicles, right adrenal MP = 83.4%, left adrenal MP = 85.1%, kidneys completely segmented, right kidney 3.4% (MP = 86.7%) with 5.8 (4–9) segments, left kidney 3.7% (MP = 89.6%) with 5.5 (4–8) segments, kidney overalp 0.13, kidney-vent interval 15.0%, kidney-vent gap 8.6%, rectal caecum 1.5%, 1–2 times the diameter of adjacent small intestine, caecum-vent interval 7.0%.

Left lung complex and tracheal lung lacking, trachea 17.8% (MP = 10.0%) with an estimated 153.8 (140– 162) tracheal rings or 85.8 rings/10% SVL, anterior tip of lung 16.8%, cardiac lung 2.0%, tracheal entry terminal, right lung 35.7% (MP = 36.7%) with one layer of large circular or oval faveoli cranially, caudal portion of lung with trabecular parenchyma forming reticulate pattern, posterior lung tip 54.6%, bronchus 17.7%, posterior tip of bronchus 36.5%, bronchus = 0.53 right lung, trachea-bronchus 35.4% (MP = 18.8%), total lung 37.7% (MP = 35.7%).

Reproduction probably in fall as MCZ 52190 (SVL 109 mm , MBD 1.5 mm ) with no ova on 2 March 1951 but MCZ 53330 (SVL 119 mm , MBD = 1.8 mm ) with 3 vitellogenic ova (2 x 1 mm ) in left ovary (right ovary missing) on 4–13 July 1950 .

Leptotyphlops burii
(
mean based on two females , BMNH 1937.11 .1.10, MZUF 4472 )

Posterior tip of sternohyoideus 8.1%, sternohyoideus-heart gap 0.40, heart 4.3% (MP = 16.0%), snout-heart interval 18.1%, heart-liver gap 6.6%, liver lobes multipartite, right liver 37.0% (MP = 43.2%) with 55.5 (53– 58) segments, left liver 31.1% (MP = 43.2%) with 51.5 (49–54) segments, anterior liver extension 0.08, posterior liver tail 0.08, left liver = 0.84 right liver, liver-gall bladder gap 0.8%, gall bladder 1.2% (MP = 63.0%) wedged between caudal pancreas (1.2%) and craniad spleen and juxtasplenic body (1.6%), right ovary 3.5% (MP = 77.6%) with 1.5 (1–2) ova and 9.5 (7–12) follicles, left ovary 2.3% (MP = 79.0%), with 2.0 (2–2) ova and 6.0 (5–7) follicles, right adrenal MP = 81.0%, left adrenal MP = 82.2%, kidneys multipartite, with appearance of a string of oval spheroids, right kidney 3.3% (MP = 83.7%) with 6.5 (6–7) segments, left kidney 3.5% (MP = 86.7%) with 7.0 (7–7) segments, kidney overlap 0.05, kidney-vent interval 18.0%, kidney-vent gap 11.6%, rectal caecum 1.4%, 2–3 times the diameter of adjacent small intestine, caecum-vent interval 10.9%.

Respiratory system lacking tracheal lung and left lung complex, trachea 17.3% (MP = 9.4%), estimated number of tracheal rings 214.5 (209–220) or 123.4 rings/10% SVL, anterior tip of lung 16.1%, cardiac lung 2.0%, tracheal entry terminal, right lung 24.8% (MP = 30.5%), cranial 4/10 with two tiers of diamond-shaped faveoli with knobs of smooth muscle at faveolar junctions, mesial 1/4 with ediculae, caudal 1/3 reticulate with trabeculae, posterior lung tip 42.8%, bronchus 15.9%, posterior tip of bronchus 33.9%, bronchus = 0.64 right lung, trachea-bronchus 33.1% (MP = 17.4%), total lung 26.7 (MP = 29.5%).
Reproduction. BMNH 1937.11.1.10 (SVL 167 mm , MBD 2.6 mm ) with 2/1 vitellogenic ova and 7/12 follicles in September, 1936.

Leptotyphlops cairi
(
mean based on three males ,
CAS 18144
, FMNH 64030 , USNM 130459 , and four females ,
CAS 18148
, FMNH 64038 , 129894 , NMZB 15419 )

Posterior tip of sternohyoideus 9.3%, sternohyoideus-heart gap 0.21, heart 3.5% (MP = 13.5%), snout-heart interval 15.3%, heart-liver gap 8.0%, liver lobes multipartite, right liver 38.2% (MP = 42.4%), with 50.0 (40– 59) segments, left liver 32.3% (MP = 39.4%) with 42.6 (38–50) segments, postcava straight, no anterior liver extension (tips symmetrical), posterior liver tail 0.16, left liver = 0.83 right liver, liver-gall bladder gap 1.6%, gall bladder 1.1% (MP = 63.6%) wedged between caudal pancreas (1.7%) and cranial spleen (0.7%) with juxtasplenic body (1.8%), pancreas with thin splenic limb, testes multipartite, right testis 5.7% (MP = 79.2%) with 12.0 (11–13) segments, left testis 5.6% (MP = 83.9%) with 13.0 (10–16) segments, right ovary 4.5% (MP = 79.3%) with 2.3 (1–5) ova and 14.7 (10–22) follicles, left ovary 3.9% (MP = 80.4%) with 2.3 (1–5) ova and 12.7 (5–21) follicles, right adrenal MP = 83.7%, left adrenal MP = 85.1%, kidneys compact but segmented, right kidney 4.0% (MP = 87.6%) with 6.0 (4–8) segments, left kidney 3.6% (MP = 90.2%) with 6.7 (5–9) segments, kidney-vent interval 14.4%, kidney-vent gap 8.0%, kidney overlap 0.23, rectal caecum 1.5%, 2.5–3.5 times the diameter of adjacent small intestine, caecum-vent interval 8.0%.

Respiratory system lacking tracheal lung and left lung complex, trachea 14.5% (MP = 8.0%) with an estimated 151.5 (119–196) tracheal rings or 103.7 rings/10% SVL, anterior tip of lung 13.2%, cardiac lung 2.1%, tracheal entry terminal, right lung 21.9% (MP = 26.2%), cranial 1/3–1/2 thick-walled with 3 layers of diamond-shaped faveolar parenchyma, caudal 1/2–2/3 tapered and reticulate with trabecular parenchyma, posterior tip of lung 37.1%, bronchus 15.0%, posterior tip of bronchus 30.2%, bronchus = 0.71 right lung, tracheabronchus 29.5% (MP = 15.5%), total lung 24.0% (MP = 25.2%).

Reproduction. Wallach (1998b) estimated the clutch size as 2–10. NMZB 15419 (SVL 182 mm , MBD 2.75 mm ) with 5/5 vitellogenic ova (1.5–1.25 x 0.75 mm ) in April, 1953; CAS 18148 (SVL 200 mm , MBD 2.5 mm ) with 1/1 vitellogenic ova (1 x 0.5 mm /1.5 x 0.75 mm ) and 5/10 follicles in April, 1955; FMNH 64038 (SVL 204 mm , MBD 2.5 mm ) with 1/1 vitellogenic ova (1 x 0.5 mm ) on 4 December 1950 .
Everted hemipenis of CAS 18144 and 18145: 3.5 subcaudals in length, nude, lacking ornamentation with enlarged bulbous base and filiform distal portion with slight taper towards apex, sulcus simple.

Leptotyphlops erythraeus
(
based on one male , MZUF 30109 )

Posterior tip of sternohyoideus 13.7%, sternohyoideus-heart gap 0.05, heart 4.3% (MP = 16.7%), snout-heart interval 18.8%, heart-liver gap 8.6%, liver lobes multipartite, right liver 31.0% (MP = 42.9%) with 59 segments, left liver 21.6% (MP = 43.3%) with 42 segments, anterior liver extension 0.16, posterior liver tail 0.14, left liver = 0.70 right liver, liver-gall bladder gap 2.7%, gall bladder 1.6% (MP = 62.0%), adjacent to slightly larger pancreas (2.0%) and juxtasplenic body (2.0%), testes multipartite, right testis 5.5% (MP = 77.3%) with 11 segments, left testis 4.7% (MP = 83.1%) with 13 segments, right adrenal MP = 80.4%, left adrenal MP = 85.1%, kidneys smooth, segmented and compact, right kidney 4.3% (MP = 85.7%) with 6 segments, left kidney 3.5% (MP = 89.2%) with 5 segments, kidney-vent interval 16.5%, kidney-vent gap 9.0%, kidney overlap 0.05, rectal caecum 1.6%, 3 times the diameter of adjacent small intestine, caecum-vent interval 9.8%.

Respiratory system lacking tracheal lung and left lung complex, trachea 18.0% (MP = 9.8%), estimated number of tracheal rings 160 or 85.0 rings/10% SVL, anterior lung tip 16.5%, cardiac lung 2.4%, tracheal entry terminal, right lung 22.4% (MP = 30.0%), cranial 1/5 with 2 layers of faveoli, middle 1/2 with single tier of ediculae, and caudal 1/3 reticulate with network of trabeculae, posterior tip 41.2%, bronchus 14.1%, posterior tip of bronchus 32.9%, bronchus = 0.63 right lung, trachea-bronchus 32.2% (MP = 16.9%), total lung 24.7% (MP = 28.8%).

Leptotyphlops ionidesi
(
mean based on male holotype ,
MCZ 50069
, male paratype ,
MCZ 18179
, and three female paratypes ,
MCZ 18181
, 57440 , 59175 )

Sternohyoideus posterior tip 13.9%, sternohyoideus-heart gap 0.07, heart 4.3% (MP = 17.1%), snout-heart interval 19.3%, heart-liver gap 6.6%, liver lobes multipartite, right liver 39.4% (MP = 45.6%) with 37.8 (35– 45) segments, left liver 27.9% (MP = 44.4%) with 35.2 (32–39) segments, anterior liver extension 0.11, posterior liver tail 0.18, left liver = 0.71 right liver, liver-gall bladder gap 2.3, gall bladder 1.4% (MP = 68.2%) adjacent to pancreas (1.4%) and caudad of large juxtasplenic body (2.2%) and spleen (1.1%), the latter with a connecting limb from pancreas, testes multipartite, right testis 4.6% (MP = 78.9%) with 7 segments, left testis 5.6% (MP = 83.0%) with 9 segments, right ovary 3.7% (MP = 82.9%) with 1.5 (1–2) vitellogenic ova and 6.7 (5–8) follicles, left ovary 2.8% (MP = 85.6%) with 1.0 (0–2) ova and 3.3 (0–5) follicles, right adrenal MP = 85.1%, left adrenal MP = 87.5%, kidneys multipartite, right kidney 3.5% (MP = 90.3%), with 6.8 (6–8) segments, left kidney 3.7% (MP = 92.6%), with 6.3 (6–7) segments, kidney overlap 0.23, kidney-vent interval 11.5%, kidney-vent gap 5.6%, rectal caecum 1.1%, equal in diameter to that of adjacent small intestine, caecum-vent interval 8.6%.

Tracheal lung and left lung complex absent, trachea 18.3% (MP = 10.1%), estimated number of tracheal rings 148.0 (126–156) or 80.6 rings/10% SVL, anterior tip of lung 17.3%, cardiac lung 2.0%, tracheal entry terminal, right lung narrow, 24.5% (MP = 31.5%) with one layer of faveoli anteriorly, posterior lung narrowly constricted and reticulate, posterior tip of right lung 43.7%, bronchus 15.8%, bronchus posterior tip 35.1%, bronchus = 0.66 right lung, trachea-bronchus 18.0% (MP = 43.7%), total lung 26.5% (MP = 30.5%).

Reproduction: MCZ 57440 (SVL 130 mm , MBD 2.4 mm ) with 2/1 enlarged vitellogenic ova (2.5 x 1, 2 x 0.75 mm / 2 x 1 mm ) and 0/5 follicles on 27 April 1958 ; MCZ 59175 lacking vitellogenic ova but with 5/8 follicles on 3 June 1958 ; MCZ 18181 (SVL 109 mm , MBD 2 mm ) with 0/2 enlarged ova (2 x 1 mm ) and 5/7 follicles on 1 August 1918 . Based on these data, eggs are laid in May and a bianual cycle may be present with a second clutch in the fall.

Leptotyphlops longicaudus
(
mean based on three males ,
CAS 85839, MCZ 51038
, TM 62060 , and three females , NMZB 1400 , 2967 , TM 57956 )

Posterior tip of sternohyoideus 13.9%, sternohyoideus-heart gap 0.10, heart 3.9% (MP = 17.4%), snout-heart interval 19.3%, heart-liver gap 6.5%, liver lobes multipartite with rectangular segments, right liver 35.6% (MP = 43.6%) with 41.3 (32–48) segments, left liver 30.2% (MP = 41.4%) with 35.2 (28–41) segments, postcava straight along cranial liver and zig-zag along caudal liver, anterior liver extension variable, on right lobe (3), left lobe (2), or symmetrical (1), posterior liver tail 0.14, left liver = 0.84 right liver, no liver-gall bladder gap, gall bladder 1.2% (MP = 62.0%), wedged between caudal pancreas and craniad juxtasplenic body, all of equal size, with smaller spleen adjacent to juxtasplenic body, testes multipartite, right testis 7.8% (MP = 70.1%) with 9.5 (9–10) segments, left testis 4.9% (MP = 75.3%) with 11.0 (8–14) segments, right ovary 4.9% (MP = 78.8%) with 2.0 (0–4) ova and 10.5 (6–15) follicles, left ovary 3.2% (MP = 82.8%) with 0.7 (0–2) ova and 6.0 (4–8) follicles, right adrenal MP = 78.3%, left adrenal MP = 80.3%, kidneys smooth, segmented and compact, right kidney 3.9% (MP = 81.6%) with 6 segments, left kidney 4.3% (MP = 85.7%) with 6 segments, kidney-vent interval 20.4%, kidney-vent gap 12.2%, kidney overlap 0.01, rectal caecum 2.3%, 2–3 times the diameter of adjacent small intestine, caecum-vent interval 12.3%.

Respiratory system lacking tracheal lung and left lung complex, trachea 18.2% (MP = 10.2%), estimated number of tracheal rings 190.0 (160–272) or 103.1 rings/10% SVL, anterior lung tip 17.6%, cardiac lung 1.8%, tracheal entry terminal, right lung 30.3% (MP =34.5%), cranial half with 1–2 layers of large diamondshaped faveoli, caudal half reticulate with network of trabeculae, posterior tip 49.6%, bronchus 17.1%, posterior tip of bronchus 36.4%, bronchus = 0.58 right lung, trachea-bronchus 35.2% (MP = 18.8%), total lung 32.1% (MP = 33.6%).

Reproduction. Broadley (1959) reported 2 elongate eggs (21 x 4 mm ) from a 178 mm SVL female (NMSR 1400) on 22 November 1957 . Wallach (1998b) estimated the clutch size as 2–6. NMZB 1400 (SVL 170 mm , MBD 3.5 mm ) with two large eggs (20 x 4, 21 x 3.5) on 22 November 1957 . TM 57956 (SVL 178 mm , MBD 4 mm ) with small vitellogenic ova (1.5 x 1 mm , 1.25 x 1 mm ) on 23 July 1984 . NMZB 2967 (SVL 202 mm ) lacking vitellogenic ova in May, 1959.

paragraph.materialsCitation:
mean based on five males , BH uncat., CAS 105928 , 105400 , FMNH 21033 ,
MCZ 74163, NMW 17131
–5, and six females , CAS 105926 , 105930 , 157118 , FMNH 26355 , MVZ 200827 )

subSubSection.paragraph: Posterior tip of sternohyoideus 10.9%, sternohyoideus-heart gap 0.18, heart 3.8% (MP = 15.2%), snout-heart interval 17.1%, heart-liver gap 7.0%, liver convoluted with multipartite, subtriangular segments having their posterior apices overlapping their neighbors, vena cava zig-zag, right liver 42.0% (MP = 45.1%) with 54.3 (34–68) segments, left liver 28.6% (MP = 40.8%) with 41.2 (16–71) segments, postcaval vein undulating between liver lobes, anterior liver extension 0.06, posterior liver tail 0.26, left liver = 0.68 right liver, liver-gall bladder gap 3.1%, gall bladder 1.0% (MP = 69.7%) adjacent to or craniad of pancreas (1.4%), caudad of juxtasplenic body (1.7%) and cranialmost spleen (0.7%), pancreas with splenic limb, testes multipartite, right testis 4.9% (MP = 80.9%) with 15.6 (8–21) segments, left testis 3.9% (MP = 83.9%) with 14.8 (7–18) segments, right ovary 2.9% (MP = 82.1%) with 1.6 (1–3) ova and 4.4 (4–5) follicles, left ovary 2.9% (MP = 84.7%) with 1.7 (1–4) ova and 2.8 (1–6) follicles, right adrenal MP = 84.7%, left adrenal MP = 86.9%, kidneys compact but segmented, right kidney 3.5% (MP = 88.9%) with 6.6 (4–9) segments, left kidney 3.7% (MP = 91.6%) with 6.0 (3–9) segments, kidney-vent interval 12.8%, kidney-vent gap 6.6%, kidney overlap 0.18, rectal caecum vestigial (0.7%), tapering cranially and less than or equal to diameter to adjacent small intestine, caecumvent interval 7.3%.

Respiratory system lacking tracheal lung and left lung complex, trachea 16.3% (MP = 9.0%), estimated number of tracheal rings 227.2 (193–290) or 138.8 rings/10% SVL, anterior lung tip 15.4%, cardiac lung 1.7%, tracheal entry terminal, right lung 20.7% (MP = 27.5%), cranial half with two tiers of thick-walled faveolar parenchyma, caudal half narrow and reticulate with network of trabeculae, posterior tip 37.9%, bronchus 14.4%, posterior tip of bronchus 37.9%, bronchus = 0.71 right lung, trachea-bronchus 30.7% (MP = 16.2%), total lung 22.4% (MP = 26.6%).

Reproduction. Wallach (1998b) estimated the clutch size as 1–7. BH uncat. (SVL 140 mm , MBD 1.5 mm ) with 1/0 vitellogenic ovum (2.5 x 0.75 mm ) and 3/5 follicles on 5 April 199; CAS 105930 with 4/3 vitellogenic ova and 1/0 follicle on 7 May 1967 ; CAS 105926 (SVL 196 mm , MBD 2.3 mm ) with 2/2 ova (1.5 x 0.75 mm ) and 2/4 follicles on 7 May 1967 ; CAS 157118 (SVL 162 mm , MBD 1.5 mm ) with 1/1 ova (5 x 1 mm ) and 1/4 follicles on 28 September 1982 ; MVZ 200827 (SVL 130 mm ) with 1/ 1 eggs (3.5 x 0.75 mm ) and 4/5 follicles (date unknown) and FMNH 26355 (SVL 216 mm ) with 1/ 1 eggs (4.0 x 1 mm /4.5 x 1 mm ) and 6/ 4 follicles (date unknown).

Leptotyphlops nursii
(
mean based on two males , FMNH 66157 , 66159 , and four females , BMNH 1905.11 .7.40, CAS 135251 , 140392 , FMNH 66158 )

Posterior tip of sternohyoideus 12.1%, sternohyoideus-heart gap 0.15, heart 3.9% (MP = 16.1%), snout-heart interval 18.1%, heart-liver gap 7.5%, liver lobes multipartite, right liver 39.1% (MP = 45.1%), with 46.0 (29– 66) segments, left liver 32.2% (MP = 44.5%) with 36.6 (25–52) segments, anterior liver extension 0.08, posterior liver tail 0.11, left liver = 0.82 right liver, liver-gall bladder gap 2.4%, gall bladder 1.2% (MP = 67.6%) wedged between caudal pancreas (1.4%) and elongate cranial juxtasplenic body (1.9%), spleen not apparent, testes multipartite, right testis 3.0% (MP = 80.5%) with 9.5 (9–10) segments, left testis 3.3% (MP = 82.0%) with 8.0 (8–8) segments, right ovary 2.7% (MP = 80.3%) with 2.3 (0–6) ova and 8.0 (7–10) follicles, left ovary 2.3% (MP = 79.5%) with 0.7 (0–2) ova and 6.3 (6–7) follicles, right adrenal MP = 83.3%, left adrenal MP = 84.6%, kidneys segmented with single renal artery, right kidney 3.5% (MP = 85.7%) with 8.0 (5–10) segments, left kidney 3.7% (MP = 88.5%) with 7.0 (4–9) segments, kidney-vent interval 16.1%, kidney-vent gap 9.7%, kidney overlap 0.15, rectal caecum 1.5%, 3–4 times the diameter of adjacent small intestine, caecum-vent interval 10.2%.

Respiratory system lacking tracheal lung and left lung complex, trachea 17.2% (MP = 9.5%) with an estimated 193.3 (160–270) tracheal rings or 112.8 rings/10% SVL, anterior tip of lung 16.2%, cardiac lung 1.8%, tracheal entry terminal, right lung 24.7% (MP = 30.4%), cranial third with 2 layers of faveoli, mesial third trabecular, caudal third of lung expanded and avascular, posterior tip of lung 42.7%, bronchus 16.8%, posterior tip of bronchus 35.6%, bronchus = 0.68 right lung, trachea-bronchus 33.9% (MP = 17.8%), total lung 26.5% (MP = 29.5%).
Reproduction. Wallach (1998b) estimated the clutch size as 7. FMNH 66158 (SVL 172 mm , MBD 2.75 mm ) with vitellogenic ova on 20 January 1951 . CAS 135251 lacking enlarged ova before May, 1972.

Leptotyphlops tanae
(
mean based on male holotype ,
MCZ 40103
, and paratype ,
MCZ 40093
)

Posterior tip of sternohyoideus 16.0%, sternohyoideus-heart gap 0.03, heart 4.4% (MP = 18.7%), snout-heart interval 20.8%, heart-liver gap 10.2%, liver lobes multipartite, right liver 39.7% (MP = 50.8%), with 37.0 (34–40) segments, left liver 35.6% (MP = 49.1%) with 34.0 (30–38) segments, anterior liver extension on left (0.015) or right (0.03) lobe, posterior liver tail 0.10, left liver = 0.90 right liver, liver-gall bladder gap 1.5%, gall bladder 1.5% (MP = 72.8%) craniad of pancreas (1.7%), testes multipartite, right testis 4.7% (MP = 87.2%) with 10.0 (9–11) segments, left testis 4.1% (MP = 88.9%) with 11.0 (10–12) segments, right adrenal MP = 86.9%, left adrenal MP = 88.0%, kidneys compact but segmented, right kidney 3.5% (MP = 90.4%) with 5 segments, left kidney 3.8% (MP = 92.3%) with 6 segments, kidney-vent interval 11.4%, significant kidney overlap (0.34), kidney-vent gap 5.9%, rectal caecum 1.2%, equal in diameter to adjacent small intestine, caecum-vent interval 7.3%.

Respiratory system lacking tracheal lung and left lung complex, trachea 19.7% (MP = 11.0%) with an estimated 149.0 (148–150) tracheal rings or 75.7 rings/10% SVL, anterior tip of lung 19.1%, cardiac lung 1.8%, tracheal entry terminal, right lung 33.6% (MP = 37.6%), narrow with one layer of edicular parenchyma, posterior lung very narrow, posterior tip of lung 54.4%, bronchus 19.5%, posterior tip of bronchus 40.3%, bronchus = 0.60 right lung, trachea-bronchus 39.2% (MP = 20.7%), total lung 35.3% (MP = 36.8%).

Everted hemipenis of MCZ 40108 (SVL 76 mm , tail 7 mm , MBD 1.2 mm ): organ single, filiform, 3 subcaudals in length ( 1.2 mm long, 0.1 mm diameter), basal 2/5 with slight expansion and a pair of lateral flaps or flanges, distal 3/5 nude with slightly tapering stem, apical surface expanded like a musical mouthpiece, sulcus simple and straight, extending along posterior surface of organ.
